blocks.body: Sentence Transformations. Eight linked lessons help you rewrite meaning through tense, modality, voice, clause links, quantity, and emphasis.

{"level":"B1-C1","intro":"In this branch you learn to rewrite a sentence through a different grammar pattern while keeping the same message, logic, and exam focus.","rules":[{"label":"KEEP THE MEANING","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the meaning you must preserve: time, certainty, condition, reason, emphasis, or quantity. The keyword only helps after that.","example":"The message stays the same even when the grammar changes."},{"label":"LOOK FOR THE SIGNAL","color":"blue","text":"Most sentence transformations contain a signal: a time marker, a modal meaning, a passive focus, a linking word, an inversion trigger, or a contrast between countable and uncountable nouns.","example":"since, unless, must, by, no sooner, a little"},{"label":"CHECK THE WHOLE FORM","color":"amber","text":"After rewriting, check the auxiliary, verb form, preposition, pronoun, article, and word order. The final mistake is often a small one.","example":"have been told / no mistakes / only a few / where he was"}]}

blocks.heading: 1. What This Branch Includes
blocks.body:
The opening Sentence Transformations branch stays flat and practical: one category and eight direct lessons in a natural order for review and exam-style practice.

  1. Sentence Transformations — Tenses - keeping time logic during tense-based rewrites.
  2. Sentence Transformations — Modals - ability, obligation, advice, deduction, and common modal paraphrases.
  3. Sentence Transformations — Passive - active-to-passive rewrites with correct tense control.
  4. Sentence Transformations — Reported Speech - statements, questions, commands, and control of tense backshift.
  5. Sentence Transformations — Conditionals - equivalents with unless, wish, would rather, and had better.
  6. Sentence Transformations — Relative Clauses / Linking Words - joining, reducing, and reshaping clauses.
  7. Sentence Transformations — Articles / Quantifiers - articles, quantity, and countable-versus-uncountable traps.
  8. Sentence Transformations — Word Order / Emphasis - inversion, clefts, emphatic do, and controlled word order changes.

{"title":"2. How to Work with This Branch","items":["First decide what cannot change: time, obligation, reason, condition, quantity, or sentence focus.","Then identify the new grammar frame: passive, reported speech, a linking word pattern, a modal paraphrase, inversion, or an article / quantifier pattern.","After rewriting, check the small forms: tense, auxiliary, pronoun, preposition, article, agreement, and word order.","At the end of each lesson, keep a short exam checklist: did the meaning stay the same, did the keyword work, and does the sentence sound natural?"]}
